mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Optimize activation of bundles with no inter-bundle path overlap
mission.description: Optimize activation of bundles with no inter-bundle path overlap. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
helm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
## What is the underlying problem you're trying to solve?

In our production environment, we have noticed that OPA bundle activation would take up to 20 minutes with only 5 bundles (2 data bundles with `data.json` only and 3 policy bundles with Rego

steps.title: Apply the fix for Optimize activation of bundles with no inter-bundle path…
steps.description:
### Why the changes in this PR are needed?

When multiple bundles are loaded into OPA using the bundle plugin, it takes a long time to active the bundles according to the logs. Profiling results has shown that most of the activation time is spent in checking path conflicts.

This PR avoids
```yaml
$ tar tzf data-bundle.tar.gz
/.manifest             <- "roots": ["foo/bar"]
/data.json

$ tar tzf policy-bundle.tar.gz
/.manifest             <- "roots": ["foo/authz", "foo/baz"]
/foo
/foo/authz
/foo/authz/authz.rego
/foo/baz
/foo/baz/baz.rego
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Optimize activation of bundles with no inter-bundle path…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Why the changes in this PR are needed?

When multiple bundles are loaded into OPA using the bundle plugin, it takes a long time to active the bundles according to the logs. Profiling results has shown that most of the activation time is spent in checking path conflicts.

This PR avoids unnecessary path conflict checking when no root overlaps are guaranteed.
codeSnippets:
$ tar tzf data-bundle.tar.gz
/.manifest             <- "roots": ["foo/bar"]
/data.json

$ tar tzf policy-bundle.tar.gz
/.manifest             <- "roots": ["foo/authz", "foo/baz"]
/foo
/foo/authz
/foo/authz/authz.rego
/foo/baz
/foo/baz/baz.rego

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-policy-agent-opa- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
